channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: I just want a fucking AP holy shit — 10 replies — 14.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
I just want a fucking AP holy shit

Open thread • Replies: 10 • Updated: 2026-05-06 04:03 UTC

OP

I just want a fucking AP holy shit

Early replies (first 3)

62194366
>>62194319
I want a rolex i dont care if its gay and lame and entry level and cheap i just dont care. I want a blue face or green face rolex.

62194401
>>62194319
Cringe.

>>62194366
Based.

62194980
>>62194319
ayo bruh AP is deffy confirmation of making it fr fr boss moves only u a real one

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62194994
>>62194366
you just need to work hard and believe in yourself
dreams do come true trust me

62194998
>>62194980
>The absolute state of AP

62195213
>>62194319
Grow the fuck up, it's a tiny piece of stainless steel that gets ruined instantly if you wear it. The value of the actual parts is like $2k in machine time and workmanship, you're paying for the name.

62195250
>>62194366
A crackhead who lives in a homeless encampment near me offered me a near mint 1680 submariner but the back had the name of a local celebrity engraved on it and he wanted 7000. I offered $1500 but he said no.

Maybe 15 years ago i had a oyster perpetual that i wore for maybe 3 years and had financial problems and stupidly sold it for $3000

62195264
>>62194366
I got a rolex for my 18th birthday and its beat to shit with lots of scratches. Gonna have to do its 10 year service this year though and I have to make sure they understand not to fix any scratches

62195304
>>62194998
>ouchdemerde pigaram
they get what they deserve

62195326
>>62195250
>Crackhead sell stolen Rolex
>Rob the buyer of his new Rolex
>Can't tell police you got robbed and lost the obviously stolen Rolex you just bought from a crackhead
>crackhead find new sucker to sell the watch.
Infinite money glitch

item.title: Bitcoin or altcoins? — 3 replies — 12.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Bitcoin or altcoins?

Open thread • Replies: 3 • Updated: 2026-05-06 04:29 UTC

OP

If you had to pick, what would you buy right now: BTC or altcoins? I’m not seeing much ROI in alts lately, even utility or memecoins.

Early replies (first 3)

62195293
>>62195275
Bitcoin until 120k

62195300
you can't time the market I thought like >>62195293
last time, bitcoin to 70k and sell half and go into alts and then 2021 alts just fucking died for a year.

The alts that pumped are things that haven't gone parabolic yet, or big projects like Solana with glowie money attached to them. Ones like Link are not going to 1000 regardless of cope.

/biz/ will pretty much only give you bad info too. I've found maybe 3 coins here that were Guaranteed returns, everything else I had to research myself. In fact I remember last year posting proof that CRCL stock was going to 10x on IPO and got XRP schizos fudding me out of taking a life changing position.

I actually hate the fuck out of baggies.

62195373
>>62195293
Why 120k only?? Why not 1M?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

No additional replies in the latest window.

item.title: Day 292 of snailposting every day until LINK is at $100 — 7 replies — 11.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
Day 292 of snailposting every day until LINK is at $100

Open thread • Replies: 7 • Updated: 2026-05-06 04:07 UTC

OP

Day 292 of snailposting every day until LINK is at $100

62191621
>>62191618
Keep going buddy, we believe in you

62191745
>>62191618
go chainlink snail!

62191750
>>62191618
cute

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62192270
keep on beliebing

https://youtu.be/AvWAURbr9fo?t=45

62194140
include the price change since the last post

62194190
Yeah

62195335
fuck stinks

item.title: No one is talking about how there's no jobs. — 77 replies — 24.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
No one is talking about how there's no jobs.

Open thread • Replies: 77 • Updated: 2026-05-06 01:33 UTC

OP

No one is talking about how there's no jobs. Ive been working for 10 years and never seen the market nearly this fucked up. Every job is fake, they will interview for 3 or 4 rounds and still repost the job next week, after ghosting people of course. People irl are coping so hard. Im back for my masters and not one person has any job or internship in any of my classes this or previous semester except me(and I will get laid off soon). These zoomers are so fucking deluded. They cant get an internship so they say "of well, im just gonna focus on finishing my masters". Is this how THEY are getting away with it? Society has already brainwashed people so badly the people in charge can drink their milkshake and leave them destitute and they wont even notice?

Early replies (first 3)

62184289
>>62184286
skill issue. ggf &c

62184301
>>62184289
All these interviews just happened to not like me or anyone else they interviewed and reposted the job? What a coincidence. Crazy how i had skills for the past 10 years and always instantly found a job but something randomly happened and now I lost whatever skills I have as I got good excperince in my fields

62184315
>>62184301
Oh and here is the email from my on-site interview on may 4th i got on friday. Nevermind it was my drram company and this tore my fucking hear out. Skill issue on my part that they canceled the role..

Oh and this is only 2/3 of all my interviews this past months if u count phone calls

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62187055
>>62186168
>"full employment" meant that EVERYONE who wanted a job had one
It has never meant that though. It was obvious as soon as unemployment numbers started being collected that there was a baseline level of unemployment that was unavoidable without resorting to slavery.
Also "wanted" is doing a lot of heavy lifting in your claim there without some denotation as to the intensity of the "want". Either you knew this but hoped nobody else would notice, in which case you're a disengenuous little shit, or you didn't notice it in which case you're at least moderately stupid.

62187058
>>62186790
Cemeteries are full of men who were certain they were irreplaceable.

62187063
>>62184556
My best friend worked in multiple industries for said cross domain knowledge, but not for a sad 90k in Muttmerica, but 200k in Europe (equivalent to 800k in Muttmerica, but something like 70k net in socialist Europe). Recently unemployed due to the IT company going bust, he exhausted his network, zero job interviews.

>>62186790
Clearly the people on top are not replaceable due to their superhuman skills.
Don't even try, I have a company doing something specialized for luxury real estate. My mother was a social worker dealing with retardation, her clients were way more skilled and grown up than mine are today.

62187071
>>62184286
Men are checking out of society at an alarming rate and the trend will only continue.

62187078
>>62187071
And that is very welcomed by everyone who is rich. Less competition, more resources, more fun in life. Check out and get marginalized in a pod. Die early, unnoticed.

62187106
>>62184286
Just say you're trans. Once hired you can say you're de transing. Should give you a job in 2 weeks.

62187112
>>62187106
Relevant already a few years ago

62187120
>>62187063
>Clearly the people on top are not replaceable due to their superhuman skills.
i work at a fortune 10 company. the board fired the CFO and CEO after these "superhumans" blew up the price by being retarded and incompetent. they were replaced in a matter of months. no one is irreplaceable. business talent is largely a retarded meme.

62187124
>>62184366
Why can’t you spell? Are you drunk because you didn’t get the job at the vapourware company?

62187126
>>62187120
**blew up the stock price

62188258
>>62185827
die-versity hire, they're probably looking for a nig of some sort. Probably not an indian though or else they'd have filled it lol.

Or perhaps you're flagged on some sort of secret 'no-hire' list for being edgy online or whatever. My workplace has a secret 'do not rehire' list that the employees aren't supposed to know about, and the way we found out was that the woman who resigned was flagged for it, she was one of the last people us coworkers would have predicted was tagged for it.

62188279
>>62186790
if the governments of the world totally shut off immigration to suit this reality, i'd not be concerned, the problem is they want to import an endless legion of turd world subhumans into the west at the same time as there's no real jobs left for the citizens. Whilst promoting their globohomo bloodsports in Ukraine and the ME.

>come white man, enlist! we only let you get the gig when we need goyim to die for lsraeI! heres you're chance!

62188591
>>62184289
>skill issue.
reddit post.

62188606
>>62184286
>they will interview for 3 or 4 rounds
By the way, they totally could figure it out in one interview. But they string you along for 3+ interviews because they think it's funny.

62188643
>>62184286
Just learn to prompt

62188765
>>62184556
>Reality is an IQ test, what do you think your IQ is?
So my boss must be smarter than me...that's why im retiring in 3 years and that fucker will be slaving until 80

62188796
>>62186790
Which is why those ideologies are either dumb or evil tempting.

62188815
>>62188279
It’s funny how you mix Ukraine with some jewish conspiracy.

62188832
>>62188796
No, communism is evil and everyone who promotes communism is a soulless monster.

62188926
>>62188815
>>62188832
Israeli IP

62188963
you laughed at le trade meme
hahhahahah
I can work anywhere I want, more jobs then I can shake a stick at, getting phone calls about asking if Im available for dispatch, sorry currently employed!

62190507
>>62185702
That all just sounds like bullshit

>>62187055
Huh? If the count was based on people who want a job why are you talking about slavery

62190516
>>62185827
>>62188258
Those are just an obnoxious type of ghost listing
https://www.fastcompany.com/91019872/employers-are-intentionally-leaving-job-openings-unfilled-heres-why

62190529
capitalism doesn't work
king now
kill jew
sell orc inc mik wop feminists
force abourt preg not want
free slave girls n rooms for white male engineer, everyone else 0 money
red lights gone instead put road circle small
highway have middle u turn lanes, no overpass
flibe.com free power
greenarrays.com replaces computing university school lawyer movies games net etc.
more barges n canals
monorails
trains
etc
dymaxion car burning hydrogen in stirling engine exhaust water
dam huge waterway for hydroelectric
100% white west playground :)
king all the way

62192053
>>62188815
kys shill-rat.
>>62188832
agreed, thats why i hate kikes, they invented communism in their kike-think-tanks, weaponised it and unleashed it on Russia+china, and the world. Kikes are either directly, or indirectly, behind 95% of all human suffering for the last ~200 years.

62194013
>>62184286
Everyone is talking about that - on plebbit and normie sites. This board is for retards with $3000 saved to gamble it on shitcoins dreaming of becoming a millionaire.

62194027
>>62185045
>network engineer
>CompTIA
Doubt. Even a CCNA won't get you a job.
CompTIA is basically worthless.

62194055
>>62184286
>Ive been working for 10 years

Woah, woah, woah Hold up there gramps.

I haven't seen attack ships on fire off the shoulder of Orion, but I am getting video interviews at least. The problem is usually the company will cuck out at the last minute and cancel the req before I get a chance to start. I've been between jobs now for 8 months, almost 10% of your career, and it happens quite a bit. I suggest you pick up a book. Maybe pass the 3 CFA exams. Until everyone learns to stop being faggots about AI, we're in for a bumpy ride because galaxy brains like yourself think you can automate and forget it. Some businesses will have to get fucked up first THEN they can start hiring humans again

62194083
>>62188643
this is serious

>>62188963
you'll be poorer than you ever imagined soon

>>62190529
this. I would like to subscribe thank you

62194913
>>62194027
even tho the faggot you replied to was replying to me, I believe his story. I joined a contractor IT job and they had many clients whom I got to interact with. Every single one of them liked me and boasted about the work i did. I reached out to the one i liked most when I was ready to rejoin employment after a year break and they swooped me up with no interview or credential checking. I do not have any certs, dropped out of college since it was a meme and a scam and learned everything through projects I was doing based from my hobbies (gaming).
>certs, degrees, etc are useless in clown world
>proof of work always works, like BTC if your proof of work is amazing YOU WILL BE SOUGHT OUT
>after a year of interacting with AIs, i asked them to give me an IQ estimate based upon our interactions and all of them told me 135+
While you are worrying about wasting time "meeting" their requirements for work, employers are wasting time meeting my requirements to work for them. You and I are not the same

item.title: >IS- IS THAT... A CHAIR!?!? >NO! NUH UH. YOU CAN'T JUST SIT DOWN WHILE WORKING! WHEN I SAID TO "WORK SMARTER NOT HARDER"… — 66 replies — 18.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
>IS- IS THAT... A CHAIR!?!? >NO! NUH UH. YOU CAN'T JUST SIT DOWN WHILE WORKING! WHEN I SAID TO "WORK SMARTER NOT HARDER"…

Open thread • Replies: 66 • Updated: 2026-05-06 01:10 UTC

OP

>IS- IS THAT... A CHAIR!?!?
>NO! NUH UH. YOU CAN'T JUST SIT DOWN WHILE WORKING! WHEN I SAID TO "WORK SMARTER NOT HARDER", I DIDNT MEAN LIKE THIS!!!!
>WHAT DO YOU MEAN YOURE TIRED!? THATS WHAT YOUR DESIGNATED 30 MINUTE BREAK IS FOR!!! WORK IS SUPPOSED TO BE AN 8 HOUR EXERCISE OF MISERY! THATS JUST HOW IT IS!!!
>D-DID YOU JUST SAY THAT IT DOESNT EFFECT YOUR EFFICIENCY!?!? DID YOU JUST POINT OUT THAT ITS 2AM AND THERE ARE NO GUESTS TO GIVE A SHIT?? THIS CANT BE HAPPENING!!!!!! THE POINT ISNT TO LOOK GOOD FOR CUSTOMERS, ITS TO UH- UM- PROVE YOUR A HARD WORKER!!!! BEING A HARD WORKER FOR ME IS THE MOST IMPORTANT THING EVER AND I SAID YOU CANT SIT WHILE YOU WORK!!!!!

Early replies (first 3)

62181647
>>62181643
I look like this and say this

62181651
>>62181643
i'm in my late 30s (white collar job etc.) but i still remember how fucking retarded retail/service industry managers were. even the district manager "elites" were insufferably fucking dumb retards.

62181656
>>62181643
Yes. Because you don't want employees to develop bad habits

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62182418
>>62182409
are we human or are we dancer

62182427
>>62182402
Idk, manager was mad he was sitting. What did the kid do after?
You can sit in private imo, just not in public.

62182448
>>62182409
Then how come 90% of people are poor?

62182449
>>62181824
Great, as soon as you slip up you're fired, thanks bye

62182461
>>62182449
I simply won't slip up. Brb it's time for my hourly bowel movement. Sucks for you LOL

And to think all you had to do was respect your wagies

62182475
>>62182427
What was the issue with him sitting? His pace was just the same and due to hi unusual height he was able to reach everything just fine. These were 16-24 hour shifts mind you, 100hr work weeks

62182497
Wait until the middle management shills ITT find out about Porn at Work Wednesdays

62182525
>>62182461
What gets me is when team leads make a big show when they don't have the power to write up anyone or even the fact that none of the shit is falling down on them. Some people just get off on being in "charge" of others.

62182537
>>62181666
>uses 3 rolls of toilet paper to make the seat level again
Not My Fucking Problem.

62182610
Man I always feel so comfy reading these threads from my work from home lake house, caught a big bass last Thursday while on long boring teams meeting from my dock

62182685
>>62182610
lol legit cope post

62183229
>>62181737
Lmao cope harder cuck

62184087
I think we should start banning employees from having caffeine too. It, like sitting, is too convenient and makes the work day too easy, am I right?

62184093
>>62181666
>Turns my 30 minutes toilet breaks into free leg workout
Sign me the fuck up

62184233
Why the fuck do blue collar workers get treated like shit compared to white collar ones?

I've worked all types of jobs (retail, warehouse, white collar office, etc.) and the double standards are insane. In my last warehouse job you literally could not be a single second over your break time. You could not even idle or take a breather if you finished your allotted work (because you were just faster/better at your job) so they find some other random shit for you to do. They penalize you for working harder.

Meanwhile in my current fake email job I can just leave my desk and go for a walk anytime I want. I can disappear for 30 mins to go to the grocery store. No one cares.

62185961
Fuck target wagies

62185969
>>62181734
that sounds like hell

62187378
>>62184233
You can disappear in your fake email job because your productivity doesn't need to be as rigorously monitored as the average blue collar worker. You're not trapped in a machine for 12 hours or stuck on an assembly line and need to make quotas. Your work doesn't produce anything of value, or you'd be getting your ass kicked from the moment you clock in. Just be glad you don't work for someone like Mr Jassy. Or you'd be getting your ass kicked and still producing nothing of value.

62188384
>>62185961
BASED BVLL

62188412
>>62184233
>>62187378
It depends on your boss in white collar though. I've had a boss who told me I couldn't read a book at my desk during downtime, even though I was in my own private office and no one could see me. Quit that job the next day and left them hanging for the monthly call.

62190691
>>62182016
>okay then give me my full hour long break as legally mandated so i can go home to get food

62190708
>>62182497
Board directors are gonna fuckin flippppp when they find out about Absolutely Cranking Your Shit In The Unisex Bathroom Thursdays

62191181
theres no reasons why cashiers shouldnt be able to sit at least when no customers are in line

62194587
>>62184233
Because most of those jobs are supposed to be for low trust niggers, and if you dont whip them, they eat you. Sadly when a respectable gentleman as you enters said environemnt is victim of it. The answer, is be a white collar worker. You dont take chances with blue collar workers. Its not because someone decided to be evil just one day.

62194595
>>62181647
Fuck yeah same bro. Love making the wagies miserable as a middle manager. And when you love what you do, you never work.

62194614
>>62182497
We don't talk to management about Wednesdays.

62194789
>>62182166
How so? Seriously, what is the logic behind your opinion?

62194796
>>62182427
> Sit in private
I don't think I've heard a more cucked opinion

62194801
>>62182610
Bassed
B)

62194818
>>62182202
depends on your weight

item.title: RETURN OF THE KING — 56 replies — 15.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
RETURN OF THE KING

Open thread • Replies: 56 • Updated: 2026-05-06 00:59 UTC

OP

LUNC just dropped a zero
None of you retards listened
You could of been rich
Instead you're all Sidelined for lunc szn

Early replies (first 3)

62188919
>>62188819
someone fucking post the vocaroo

62188994
>>62188819
ok, now zoom out
kek baggies

62189013
>>62188994
>t. buck broken linkie

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62192368
>>62192185
>t. link baggot

62192423
>>62192216
>I'm up $55.

62192451
>>62192423
Hey, up again, $64.
I'll totally wait to hit 1 cent. It'd be worth way more than I put in.

And, I'm up over my stock market gains for today.

62192456
>>62192451
I regret throwing half my investments at stocks. They've barely moved while my LUNC buys are up ~3x over 4 months

62192467
>>62192456
War buys?
That's what my stuff was and zero way to anticipate when Trump would say something to kill any gains or boost something you weren't touching.

See you at 1 cent

62192832
>>62192185
>It just went over the resistance
>it's not stoppping

I guess linkies are still stinkies then

62192837
Lol bought this shit years ago, I genuinely thought it was dead?

62192905
>>62192837
if you bought it when it crashed, it was never over.

CZ said he would burn coins with a protocol and it's still burning. From that point it was never dead, and once again, CZ delivers

62192915
>>62192905
Yeah, but it was half this much a week ago. It's already gained like 100%, I'm up $84 from like $8 a week back.

62193017
Haha, drop back. Hold steady bois

62193037
I'm staking this but haven't checked in years. My terra station app won't open or update. Wut do?

62193043
>>62193037
Goy here btw

62193161
>>62193037
original terra station shut down,
new terra station is being relaunched by vultsig/thorchain after acquiring.

use Keplr

62193197
>>62193037
>>62193161
My terra station app works.

62193289
>>62192216
>im up $55!

>>62192451
>im up $64!

>>62192915
>im up $84!
jfc lunc does a 3x and these are the gains people are getting excited about?

62193316
>>62188819
NIGGA YOU HOLDER LUNC
LOSER UNC SHIT NIGGA HAHAHAHAHAHAHA

62193335
>>62193289
I'm up $350 on the daily
>>62193316
My LUNC bags have outperformed 99% of crypto since January.

Stay mad seething linkie. This trades obviously not for you. See you at $119

62193503
checked back in on my staking rewards and netted a grand total SEVEN DOLLARS

62193537
>>62193503
Technically you got more, staking rewards don't show the USTC reward value earned. Which will be worth thousands when it repegs

62193550
>>62193335
I hold TOSHI nigga you don’t even know bout me shit loser unc nigga

62193557
>>62193335
I hope you enjoyed it while it lasted. It looks like the trade bots are dumping it

62193571
>>62193550
>toshi
Kek poorfag. Probably bought the top too

62193595
>>62193571
You wish nigga I bought in August 23
Probably made more money than you’ve ever had in your life too kek nigga

62193601
>>62193161
>>62193197
LET ME IN

LET ME IIIIIIIIIIIINNNNNNN!!!!!

SERIOUSLY GUYS IM SCARED ALL MY LUNC IS TOAST

62193617
it is not wise to hold a coin that the majority of people holding are hoping and praying goes up to recoup their losses on

62193760
>>62193617
It's whatever. Just don't dump a crazy amount in. $1000 or less and let it go, just take out what you put in and maybe 50% profit after a certain point. Let the rest go.

99% of these coins have been sitting so it's cool to see them wake up.

62193800
>>62193760
Lol who are you kidding?

62194434
Plans are underway to reactivate Mirror protocol
The proposal will be submitted on the eve of the Terra Luna collapse (May 12th)
Accumulation time is over.
You've been warned

62194784
>>62192173
well, its best you hold on to it before you rope when this dumps red

or either I'll rope if this does fly to the moon like what NASA did on April 1st

item.title: The US national debt is now bigger than the entire US economy Today's debt milestone is not borne from war mobilization. — 74 replies — 13.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
The US national debt is now bigger than the entire US economy Today's debt milestone is not borne from war mobilization.

Open thread • Replies: 74 • Updated: 2026-05-05 23:12 UTC

OP

The US national debt is now bigger than the entire US economy
Today's debt milestone is not borne from war mobilization. It is the accumulated result of tax cuts without matching reductions in spending, an aging population drawing more heavily on Social Security and Medicare, and years of political leadership on both sides of the aisle that has consistently chosen short-term popularity over long-term solvency.
You fags are cooked (including me)

Early replies (first 3)

62179629
Doesn’t matter, because no one can collect from the US Govt

Refute this without sounding mad

62179630
>>62179622
breaking news: voters want stuff and dont want to pay for it

62179853
>>62179622
AI will fix this

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62183104
>>62179629
>because no one can collect from the US Govt
The Federal Reserve already collects from the US government. The US is only solvent as long as they do the Feds bidding.

62183137
>>62179928
>300 million all of a sudden joining together because of some anon's stupid chest-pounding fantasy
>t. retard who's been somehow asleep the past year

62183141
>>62179622
All you have to do is cut to 2019 spending levels and we would have a surplus.
That's how crazy Congressional spending has increased since Covid.

62184058
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=aDAL5G9Xwn4&list=RDaDAL5G9Xwn4
https://i.4cdn.org/biz/1777749403133051.png

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=4ZHwu0uut3k&list=RD4ZHwu0uut3k
>[720p]
https://i.4cdn.org/biz/1777749468586523.jpg

62184159
>>62179629
Most of the US debt isn't from foriegn countries. It's US themselves. US Pension & retirement funds, US investment firms, US etfs etc. If US defaults you'll see US citizens and companies lose money. Holding US treasuriers aren't going to be worth as much since US isn't going to meet its obligations. Companies and people will lose a lot money. Contraction of the economy will happen for years due to this cycle. You'll also see a stock market crash and the decades of growth will vanish because nobody will want to lend to the US at similar rates. It'll probably be as bad as the great depression if not worse.


Hopefully this happens and I can a US citizen as a slave wagie.

62184174
>>62180399
This is not a survey of people willing to fight for their country; it's a survey of people willing to click "yes" on an internet poll. The reality would be a tiny tiny fraction of that number.

62184182
>>62184174
Dude we literally go to europe and beat the fuck out of your pickpocket niggers and muslims if they fuck with us. Realistically only 5 million Americans would be needed to beat europe down like dogs

62184190
>>62179629
The US depends on people continuing to buy its debt, just to fund ongoing expenses

62184198
>>62180442
>eliminate all taxes for people making less than ~75k a year
Uh, lower taxes won't help the debt.

>block immigration
More people = more tax revenue

62184199
>>62184190
Just like all country?

62184207
>>62184199
There are countries that run small or no deficits and could cope quite well with zero net debt issuance.

62184218
>>62180410
india has like 1.4 billion people the self-hating white obsessed ones you see in the west represent a tiny fraction the actual ones in india are all retarded jingoists or muslims

62184243
>>62184182
That's cute but every single time in the past 30 years that you guys have had an opportunity to show that you're not all talk, you've rolled over like bitches. Off the top of my head:
>allowing cops to go door to door confiscating guns during katrina
>allowing military to go door to door conducting warrantless searches after the boston marathon bombing
>hiding in your house posting on twitter while mobs of leftists took over entire cities while completely crippling police forces and burning down police stations during the george floyd riots
You lives are too comfortable to ever risk them. You have fun larping online but we all know that the only people willing to risk their lives in the real world are those with either strong convictions or little to lose and we both know that you have a lot to lose and don't really believe in anything.

The only time Americans show any balls is when there's a call for people to die for Israel.

62184279
>>62179622
>>62179629
>Doesn’t matter, because no one can collect from the US Govt
>>62179928
>Come and take the debt
funny to see americans think the debt is owed to other countries instead of its own people.

62184305
>>62184279
forgot pic related

62184336
>>62179622
>Cut defense spending
>Cut medicare
>Cut social security
>Tax rich people, tax the middle class, tax the working class, tax the poor, tax your mom. Tax your Grandma. Everyone's getting picked up, flipped over around shaken for money and needs to get over it.
>Inflate the currency
>Keep every other program because those those don't actually matter that much. In fact, add more.
>Ignore culture wars. Ignore culture clowns.
>Pay our doomer, meme-brained, low IQ, lazy and angertainment-addicted public a few pennies to go to the park to end the loneliness epidemic and polarization and just go outside and see how human beings function
>Cut the "muh affordability" meme subsidizes. We're adults. Show them the real bill and just stop it.
>Nuclear, Wind and Solar power. Shut up about the rest.
>Viciously murder zoning, car centricity and nimby laws on public television
Amendment guaranteeing that you have to let people build houses on land they buy provided it's not a safety or environmental hazard.
>Respect law outcomes, but persecute each and every corrupt official no matter what to where you make Watergate look like a peace rally compared it.
>Expose every grifter. They might not be prosecutable, but they will be known.
>Spew TRVTHS daily and nightly no matter who it hurts (including yourself)
There. Fixed your country.

Do you think the average American won't do this because they're afraid won't work or because we are afraid of seeing the infeasibility and sunk cost of the current American lifestyle?

62184343
>>62184207
Such as?

62184400
>>62179629
Eventually the dollar collapses though and bond yields spike because people demand higher and higher interest on what's a collapsing dollar

62187354
>>62184058

https://i.4cdn.org/biz/1777750871226027.jpg
https://cdn.donmai.us/sample/a2/1a/__gunsmith_bitch_panty_stocking_with_garterbelt_drawn_by_sketchinventor__sample-a21abc6dafbdafd9ed6c0893528de850.jpg

62188579
>>62181550
This
>>62179928
>Come and take the debt
You are genuinely retarded. Slavery needs to come back, all races.

62188593
>>62179622
>You fags are cooked (including me)

62188936
>>62184343
Surplus: Norway, Denmark
Minor deficit: Sweden

62192220
>>62188593
Yummy

62193530
>>62179629
Food
>America is food secure
Unti AI agriculture was the most subsidized industry in the country.

62193681
>>62184336
Just re-arrange american healthcare and social security system. Private healthcare and private education are incredibly costly.

62193707
>>62184336
The system more than likely is incapable of being fixed at this point and cutting benefits is political suicide.

62193761
>>62193707
The fix is inflation. They'll keep running it hot to inflate away the debt while forcing bond rates lower artificially.

62194225
>>62179622
Doesn't matter its all fabricated when the fed can print infinitely

62194394
>>62179629
The world stopping to use of the US dollar as the de-facto reserve currency and universal trade tender is not something the US in its current state can survive tho.

Any other country in the world would be facing the greatest hyperinflation event ever seen if they did the shit the US is doing. They're the only things barely preventing it right now.

62194405
>>62184336
you assume things aren't being played out exactly as intended

item.title: He's right you know. Stop investing in shitcoins and get rich quick scams. That doesnt exist outside anomalies like BTC… — 41 replies — 9.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
He's right you know. Stop investing in shitcoins and get rich quick scams. That doesnt exist outside anomalies like BTC…

Open thread • Replies: 41 • Updated: 2026-05-06 00:10 UTC

OP

He's right you know.

Stop investing in shitcoins and get rich quick scams. That doesnt exist outside anomalies like BTC which are rare and nobody can predict.

Pull yourself by your bootstraps, contribute 30k a year into your investment account, and watch it grow. In 10 years you'll be over millionaire and earning 100k+ in interest per year. In another 10 years you'll have over 2.5 million.

The collapse of the US market means the collapse of the world, and by that point we'll all be dead anyways.

Here's a fact, The US has #1 GDP, 32 trillion. You can say this and that, but it is the worlds largest economy, and it does the most money trading in the entire world and is the dominant force over the world.

Early replies (first 3)

62191402
He's right except with BTC instead of Boomer stocks

62191409
fr btc is a more logical value of storage in a collapsing world

62191415
>>62191402
>>62191409
>I know more about money than a guy who has 150 billion USD.
>MFW

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62191509
>>62191491
Why do you choose the 5 years? I dont give a shit about bitcoin but its the best performing asset in history if you take its whole history.

62191516
Kek

62191522
>>62191516
Didnt it actually beat BTC doebiet

62191523
>>62191493
>>62191477
It was also a time when the only people in the stock market where fellow upper class white guys. The government also pumped his bags by getting rid of pension and making everyone buy his portfolio

62191526
>>62191522
That is the Berkshire/ btc chart my dude

62191559
>>62191399
Op BRUTALLY analy raped to the point of silence. Good job boys total boomer death

62191560
>guy who owns a ton of US stocks recommends you buy his bags

62191566
>>62191526
And how many people invested in BTC back then and crypto because they had 50k or 100k laying around and took a chance. Maybe they would've got a couple million. Water is wet.

nvidia outperformed BTC in the past 10 years. And while BTC has dubious claims, nvidia actually has a real business in the future of AI and electronics.

What is BTC even used for besides being a shiny internet token lol with no intrinsic use or value that has a dubious future.


>>62191559
BTC is a shiny computer rock also post timer retard LMAO

62191576
>>62191566
All you need to do is DCA into BTC and you will outperform Berkshire and Nvidia over the next 10 years.

It doesn't matter when you start, it will outperform fiat companies earning fiatbux forever.

62191594
>>62191566
He just exposed himself as a 50 year old clueless redneck. My nigger if you invested 100k back then you would've been richer than warren himself. Good rage bait tho.

62191609
>>62191576
LMAO. If you invested in BTC within the last year you're literally losing money out the ass.

Meanwhile if you went all in on nvidia you're up 500% over the last 2 years. That means if you put 300k into nvidia it would be worth 1.5 mil

Nvidia is going to 400 a share I feel bad if you havent invested. They are the #1 provider of AI for the US military

62191615
>>62191508
>Bro, just leave the job they took 9 months to get even with a master degree to different job that is enirely out in left field
>Just work harder bro
>instacart 10 hours a day 5 days a week
I don't have a car because you know..I actually invest in stocks unlike your larp.

62191628
>>62191609
Nvidia is still at its 2016 price and most likely a local top.

This is the power of the best performing AI stock in the world kek.

62191633
>>62191399
30k at 7% annual growth while adding 30k each year is 405k in 10 years you fucking nigger.

62191686
>>62191628
Yea like I said, buy buttcorn in 2016 and be one of the 0.05% of people who had money and decided, then yeah buttcorn outperforms it.

A lot more people have got and will get richer off nvidia.

62191706
>>62191686
No one bought nvidia before it pumped.

If you bought recently you are a turbo normie and will get rekt.

62191728
There's nothing to suggest index funds will continue to only go up over time, in fact there is more evidence to the opposite.

62191755
>>62191399
This only works if you make top 10% income, the bottom 90% have to gamble or they're not going to make it

62191763
GET RICH SLOW YOU MOTHERFUCKER
GET RICH SLOW
WAIT TILL YOU DIE
AND FUCK YOU

62191808
>>62191763
HOLD MY BAGS GOY, GETTING RICH QUICK IS IMMORAL, JUST GET RICH SLOW

62191815
if I am being generous he's telling people that they don't have the chops to make it, if I'm being cynical he wants to keep retards from competing with him and keep money flowing into his broad market assets.

62191850
>>62191399
Fun fact: back in the days when they hung out Buffett convinced Gates to diversify his portfolio and buy value stocks. Gates sold most of his MSFT shares, bought shit like Canadian National Railway and Kraft Heinz, and lost a gazillion bucks.

62191851
>>62191491
What does that matter if by the time I cash out a pack of chicken legs or a tube of ground beef is $150 plus tip? The pack of chicken legs was $6 when I moved out in 2017 and now it’s $16 in less than ten years. That’s assuming you don’t pay any taxes or maker and market fees. 160% gains over ten years mean nothing if the prices double every 5 years now

62191871
>>62191628
>Nvidia is still at its 2016 price and most likely a local top.
Dafuq. They had a 4:1 stock split and 2021 and a 10:1 stock split in 2024.

62192018
>>62191851
It means alot if you minimized bills, have a paid off house, and are a work from home chad who doesn't have to use that ball and chain called a car every day.

62192038
>>62191850
Top tier old fart boomer brain. Humans really are simple creatures huh, biased to whatever the fuck they feel cozy about.

62192687
>>62191851
Im a single guy so inflation doesnt hurt me as much. Also I dont eat much either. The cost to eat mcdonalds is what 400 a month, 4800 a year? in 5-10 years it will be 6000 a year, whoopdie freaking doo.

62192768
>>62191633
Vanguard Growth ETF has an annual yield of 18% for the past 5 years.

62192782
>>62191628
>Nvidia is still at its 2016 price and most likely a local top.
You're ngmi

62194650
>>62191399
>The collapse of the US market means the collapse of the world, and by that point we'll all be dead anyways.
Idiot prefer to be dead than view a world without Capital.

item.title: The LINK token has value — 85 replies — 9.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
The LINK token has value

Open thread • Replies: 85 • Updated: 2026-05-05 19:14 UTC

OP

The LINK token has value

Early replies (first 3)

62176216
New Chainlink report
https://bitwiseinvestments.com/crypto-market-insights/chainlink-in-plain-english

62176226
>>62176214
>In many cases, nodes must stake LINK before they can provide and be compensated for their services. If they provide incorrect data, they are penalized and lose a portion of the LINK they have staked. This system helps ensure the integrity of Chainlink’s services while also
reducing the free-floating supply of LINK.
Speaking in present tense. I assume this and the and the other clues we've seen in the past couple weeks mean staking imminent?

62176244
>>62176226
What other clues

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62187773
>>62184641
not for stink holders tho

62187784
>>62184641
>>62182805
>>62184151
$9
kek baggie keep spamming your shitty videos

62188638
>>62176323
Holy copeeeee
LINKIES LOSSSST AHAHAHA MORE ADOPTION THAN MICROSOFT AND NVIDIA
50$ —> 6$

62189051
>>62177113

62190186
>>62187773
clarity act regulations, tokenization, security awareness, it's all changing in chainlinks favour at once
>>62187784
thanks! i will

62190231
>>62176910
These Jeet bot goy gold staking posts really fire me up bros

62190282
>>62190186
Are these the mechanisms that will allow chainlink token holders to sue for equity?

62190912
So... it was all fake huh?
Glad my gut instinct told me to sell

62191218
>>62190282
the only people who would try that are VC types who didn't buy early enough

62192644
>>62184641
None of the numbers have been confirmed by anybody.

62192801
>people still talking about $link
shit like polymarket is literally the perfect use-case for something like chainlink, and yet, they don't use it in any major prediction market.
why do you delusional retards still believe?

62192822
>>62192644
many numbers confirmed. you can estimate
>>62176336

62192854
>>62192822
>where is the proof from all those off-chain payments?
>look at muh cumulative graphs bro!
are you legitimately retarded?

62192867
>>62192822
How many times did Sergey drill "truth > trust" and "mathematically guaranteed" for people to completely glaze over it? Like he just diluted the concept from overspamming it.

62192888
>>62192644
>>62192854
The fuck are you talking about "confirmed", the quote is about other projects saying they're paying CLL millions. Ask them for confirmation of what THEY are saying.
Charles said something similar btw.

>>62192867
Truth > trust is about how the network works. Not CLL's internal revenues; nobody ever promised you "truth" about that.

62192901
>>62192888
Only low functioning autists and GME type of normies don't see the obvious bad optics of it.

62192907
It’s insane how obsessed fudders are with this. Every day for years.

62192909
>>62192888
so zero proof of all those off-chain payments?
the only proof that exists is Zach's anecdote from an irrelevant podcast 3 years ago?
yikes

62192925
>>62192901
Bad optics of what? Private companies having private company accounting?

>>62192909
There's plenty of proof, lots of cryptos had public DAO votes about Chainlink integration and rates.
Lots of anecdotes from projects themselves too, like Hoskinson saying CLL would charge him an "absurd number" for direct Cardano integration.

Step out of your bubble

62192926
>>62192907
I can assure you most "fudder" are high 6 figs in link.
It's you triple digit baggies thinking you'll buy a lambo soon that are deluded

62192938
>>62192926
lol @ ESL
I can assure you most fuddies are brown niggers that don't hold any LINK

62192940
>>62192925
>lots of cryptos had DAO votes
>zero cryptos mentioned
>lots of anecdotes from projects
>the only anecdote that's been repeated ad nauseam is the known scammer Hoskinson when referring to his vaporware blockchain pet project
the absolute STATE of LINK shills

62192962
>>62192938
Really going to make me dev that crypto ZKP attestation gated signal group to filter out you pot smokers.

62192982
>>62192938
we fuddies are richer than you can even possibly imagine
due to our wealth, we are basically a different species altogether
a super species that can no longer breed with inferiors such as yourself

62192984
>>62192888
>duuuuuuuude everyone else needs to be truthful except us


hey kid, did you notice your cult stopped using the ollll truth>trust after they got called out?

they grifted you, and here you are begging for their approval

THE Cuckolds of crypto

62192988
>>62192925
>duuuuuuuuude one of the top grifters in crypto history charles said my cult was expensive

there you have it folks, irrefutable truth

62193165
>>62192940
>>62192984
>>62192988

62193169
>>62176477
do you have any response to >>62176598 ?
If you don't that proves you are either
A) stupid bot
B) stupid nigerian
C) angry fudder
D) link holder trying desperately to fud others away from the best moonshot there is.
in any case, everyone can just ignore you: )

62193244
>>62192982
nigga no one is breeding with you KEK

>>62192988
or you lul

brownies rekt once again

62193356
>>62176295

item.title: Large sum into SCHD — 23 replies — 7.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
Large sum into SCHD

Open thread • Replies: 23 • Updated: 2026-05-06 01:38 UTC

OP

As an already made it strategy? Enough to almost nearly offset my cost of living. Not quite retire but close. The idea being that if I suddenly didn't have a job that it would be a fallback where I don't have to touch the principal. I know I could just put it into spy and follow the 4% rule if I ever needed to, but idk something about the dividends being close to my bare minimum just kind of feels right.

So long as I'm gainfully employed dividends could go into whatever risk asset. And I already dca my paychecks into VT.

Early replies (first 3)

62188587
>>62188581
As I'm currently in cash/treasuries I could also wait until the rates drop some more since the market is sketchy at the moment.

62188667
I wouldn't go all in one fund, but dividend growth funds that increase nav and dividends are the only kind of fund i am fine never selling. My div port yields 6% and the nav grows, mix of GPIX, amplify trio, then schd/dgro/vymi

62188740
>>62188667
>wouldn't go all in one fund
True. I could split schd and dgro

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62188899
>>62188587
>the market is sketchy at the moment
The market is *always* sketchy though.

62188992
>>62188899
Yeah

62189018
>>62188740
>schd and dgro
I've meditated on it and this is what I'll do.
Then I can focus on taking bigger risks with smaller principal and keep dumping profits into VT, schd, dgro

62189609
>>62188581
Every boomer ever failed into success.
Anyway, I own SCHD, and it is the biggest part of my self directed IRA.
But.
Look at the underlying equities.
It isn't great desu.
Should give you pause.

62189763
>>62188581
Look into NEOS covered call ETFs. They do it right and they mostly follow their underlying. I personally use them to endure crab markets. Right now holding CSHI, IAUI, BTCI, NEHI, and QQQI from them. CSHI is basically my HYSA, I unload from there any time I want to buy heavily into something else. The rest I DCA.
Other than these I hold SCHD, SCHY, USOY (bad one, has huge NAV erosion, only holding it until the war is over), URNM, and UNRJ.

62189797
>>62189609
>Look at the underlying equities.
>It isn't great desu.
I know it's why I didn't do it already. It's all trash like turbotax and spic depot. But then the sp500 is also trash.
I didn't buy mining stocks because I read the industry had mismanagement and nepotism, missed out on an easy 3x or whatever it did.

>>62189763
Nice. I'm not into covered call etf for the long term but was thinking it could make sense to just swing my portfolio, covered call for the current crab situation, instead of set and forget in some etf.

Damn now I'm rethinking it. Good thing cash is still over 3% I can take my time.

62189980
>>62189797
Covered call etfs have a bad habit of NAV erosion so make sure you do your research.

62190555
>>62189980
Not the good ones. SPYI and QQQI are following SPY and QQQ with little lag, assuming you reinvest the dividends, and assuming we will be in a bull market like 2008-2026. If we enter stagnation or a soft bear market, SPYI and QQQI will perform better over the years. If it's a hard crash, then it will perform just as bad as the underlying.

62190563
I've outperformed the funds by looking at the fundamentals in the underlying stocks of funds like SCHD in FASTGraphs and cherrypicking the ones that seem underpriced at the moment, but I am a control freak who can't handle someone else being in control of my investments even if it's an algo.

62190647
>>62190563
using etfs/overlap is a very low effort but good filter. youre usually at least getting into something you can hold a long time if it doesnt go the way you want right away

62190654
riding Be up
watching SNDK
I got EDSA at 8.9 went to 5.5 and sold out at 8.91 then it went to 19.5 and now at 16, coulda doubled my capital if I had stayed home n done 0

62190759
If you could invest 100% and go into a coma for 20 years it would be in an actively managed AI ETF and you'd come back to 50x your money.

62191660
>>62190555
>SPYI and QQQI are following SPY and QQQ with little lag, assuming you reinvest the dividends
So you're saying you're better off investing in SPY and QQQ instead of SPYI and QQQI? Interdasting take there anon.

62191678
>>62191660
for the kind of person that models a return as a pure exponential, that makes sense. start accounting for the real time value of money and its not as clearcut

62191682
>>62188667
Mind sharing your percentage in each? I’m trying to put some money aside for a dividend portfolio.

62191683
it's just a metal bro

62193306
>>62191682
If you just went equally into all those funds you would get just under 6%. I don't really have a target percentage other than I wouldn't go above 20% gpix and idvo+vymi to keep my international allocation at 20%. I don't auto-reinvest dividends, just accumulate the cash and buy what's cheaper at the time for the most part. When I'm retired, I want mostly qualified dividends so I can use my standard deductions for roth conversions. GPIX is nice, because it is largely ROC until cost basis goes to zero. I can get a big yield mostly tax free early on in retirement and use the deduction for roth converting instead.

62193906
>>62191660
Yes, if you assume we are in for another 18 years of a bull run with relatively minor hiccups.

62193930
>>62191678
>that models a return as a pure exponential
Right I think there is an advantage to cash flow. Especially tax advantaged dividends

62194925
>>62193906
No, that assumption was made in the post I was responding to, cf
>>62190555
>assuming we will be in a bull market like 2008-2026

item.title: Old guy in Corvette cope — 94 replies — 6.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
Old guy in Corvette cope

Open thread • Replies: 94 • Updated: 2026-05-05 14:26 UTC

OP

This will be YOU, just some cringe old fart finally getting his dream car after muh 401k investing for 40 years.

If you're not investing everything into Bitcoin RIGHT NOW to potentially make it in 10 years while you still have some youth left it's fucking over for you. I hope I make it live like a fucking king in my youth while you will be waging till 60 years old.

MAKE IT OR DIE

Early replies (first 3)

62181483
i would rather buy a corvette over buying bitcoin at 78K

62181491
>>62181462

No I bought my dream car 3 years ago and it got repossessed and emptied my 401k and have to declare bankruptcy. I'm only 35 though.

62181498
are we just glossing over the fact there's a fucking dinosaur in the background?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62184540
>>62181462
I have 15k in MSTR, bitcoin maxi chud, tell me, what will that be worth in 10 years ?

62184625
>>62181905
no

62184639
>>62181498
holy shit is that real

62184698
>>62183644
>V8
Pussy shit. Make it money is having a butler to drive you around with the V10. Touching a steering wheel is for the poor.

62184707
>rental
Few years pass and you would have already done a huge chunk of the payments.
RENTKEKS ARE SUBHUMANS

62185860
Got mine about a year ago. C7z06 with a clutch pedal. Every performance metric is way out of its league. Amazing gm can make something like a z06 and a Chevy equinox as well

My only complaint is the long ass gearing. It’s hard to have fun around town. You really gotta find a proper spot to do a nice 1-2-3 hit. 3rd gear tops out at like 123mph.

Also, fuck the gay ass nuvette. And also…. I’m in my fucking 40s now still waiting for the link yacht ho-down.

62185906
>>62181905
>>62181907
Inshallah

62185924
>>62185860
That looks sick man.
My ‘maro

62185929
>>62184698
Damn. Now this is what I call a proper bangwagon
>>62181462
Also, you must be an ABSOLUTE faggot if you still have shit like “muh dream car”
Car have two utilities
1- drive you
2- show women you have money

62185933
>>62183644
douchemobile

62185947
>>62181462
Is this like the pinnacle of boomer social status? A fucking meme sports car which deprecates as soon as it drives off the lot?

62185997
>>62185924
Awesome color and looking clean as hell.

62186032
>>62185924
Hell yeah brother. My wife has a 6th gen lt1. Nothing comes close to them in the price range. The alpha chassis is so good. I had a 4v blackwing prior to my z06 so I had some good seat time with an alpha chassis and a tr6060 manual.

Gonna be completely honest, I actually like the alpha chassis more than rhe c7 vette. Much easier to push hard at near the limit, and steering feels better/communicates more. Vette is obviously a bit faster around a track.

I really need to grab some 1le rep wheels for my wife’s lt1. They look sick. Good color choice too senpai.

Pic rel was my 4v blacking. I loved the car, it just should have came with a v8. I came from an f80 m3 comp prior. Both manual. 4vbw was much more dialed in… but that v6 tt was so blaaah

62186033
I didn't buy Bitcoin in 2013. Its over for me a loooong time ago.

62186039
>>62181782
>>62182527
good luck breaking 6.49 second quarter mile with a stick.

62186074
>>62186039
Good luck telling time with your Rolex

Good luck being a dj with antiquated technics 1200 turntables and vinyl records

Many people like mechanical/basic feels more. Nothing like rowing your own gears after pulling a 1.7 60’ and getting a clean pass. Some niggers just don’t get it and that’s ok

>>62186033
>he fell for the fud and hasn’t been stacking link. U have an opportunity rn that is only marginally worse than old link fags. Load the fuck up and be ready to hold another 40 years. Or 1 year.

62186078
>>62181462
My friend raced a guy in his corvette once and when we pulled upto the set of lights he wound down his window with the biggest smile and was hooting and hollering "whoooo boys good shit that was funnn!" he yelled like he was 16 again.

OP is a fag.

62186088
>>62186074
im just saying its like the bell curve meme, automatic is for women, stick is for men, automatic is for real men who go faster than 6.50

62186098
>>62186078
Can confirm
>I go to cars and coffee
>roll around in drag radials trying to get into shenanigans
I used to mock corvette owners…. But I think now I get them. To the burger American boomer, the corvette was the greatest car ever (they have Alzheimer’s concerning dodge vipers), and they feel like they “made it” when they have one to park in the garage and take out to Texas Roadhouse for a steak with the ol ball n chain.

If heard boomers say this phrase often
>vet is the greatest American sports car ever made, bar none. It’s boomers made it car
>you need yourself a got dang John deer, thats when you made it bud

62186111
>>62181462
uncmaxxing

62186115
>>62186088
Again…Real men don’t drive autos. Iv been driving for almost 25 years. Never owned an auto and there is a good chance I never do. Only car that excites me for a future purchase is a cayman gt4, dodge viper, or a 996.2 gt3 911, or a Cadillac 5v blacking. I’ll be in the back, a bit off the lead but I will be rowing my own smiling. I will also continue to dj nothing but vinyl records, and also rock automatic watches.

62186126
>>62186115
sick 'bishi brah, how much horsepower does your cabron fibre add?

62186131
>>62186039
I mean good luck breaking that with most cars wtf is this logic
>>62186098
I think car goes vrooom and he got a chance to safely push it against some youngins happy to roll along side him, no ego just fun.

62186146
>>62186126
A decent amount of downforce according to gm.

Funny to see all these new car fags..because the barrier of entry is so low (don’t even know how to operate a manual in most cases). These same niggas were cracking jokes about us being stuck in the past….. but now these Tesla plaids come out to no prep events, stripped to shit with 4 drag radials murking prepped built cars …. And now technology is gay.

These guys will never appreciate the fear of not getting your rev-matched downshift perfect as to not upset a car while entering a turn….. with the absolute risk of upsetting the car and going into a wall.

Same shit as launch and traction control. We used our right foot and muscle memory in our day sunny boi!

62189488
>>62181531
Prigozhin started Wagner PMC in his early 50s. It's not over until you give up or die.

62189709
>>62189488
I'm 42 can I still make it and get a virgin wife and g wagon by 50?

62191796
>>62181498
there is a dinosaur in the foreground

62191901
>>62186146
99% of car fags now days don't even like driving, they only care about stuff like, what material the dashboard is made of of how many screens and neon lights it has etc
and of course they want big power for status but they can't even drive

62191957
>>62185860
>C7z06 with a clutch pedal
Literally the only thing that can stand up in value to the Supra imo. Too bad about the new one and no manual.

>>62185924
>>62183644
You guys are alright, you pull on me in a straight line and I cope with muh track times, shit interiors, reliability, etc.

>>62186098
> "corvette is the best bang for buck at any price point"
> "it's a boomer world and we are just living in it"
Someone on /o/, maybe you, but that shit hit. I still don't want one except the one you have though. C05/6 looks like utter shit. Nu doesn't look as good as yours and no manual == no go. I hate people who view manuals as some magic skillset, it's not that, just more fun.

>>62189488
He started his businesses far earlier iirc. The whole school food / restaurants for the Russian elite thing.

The problem is that I whole-heartedly believe in sex being different if they actually want you vs wanting you for the money.

62192036
>>62181612

I remember a supervisor I used to work with bought a brand new truck right after a promotion. He didn’t pass the probationary period and was demoted back to his former position.

HAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAH

item.title: I REFUSE TO BELIEVE THAT $63000 WAS THE BOTTOM OF THIS CYCLE FUCKING BULLSHIT — 10 replies — 5.9/hr

Open thread • Replies: 10 • Updated: 2026-05-06 03:02 UTC

OP

I REFUSE TO BELIEVE THAT $63000 WAS THE BOTTOM OF THIS CYCLE
FUCKING BULLSHIT

Early replies (first 3)

62191529
>>62191524
Do what's the top?

62191537
Sorry you missed it.

Try again in 4 years.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62194555
>>62191524
lol

62194563
>>62191537
most bull markets only last 6 to 9 months and we are already 3 months into this one.

62194722
>>62191524
$59k was the bottom botard

62195007
>>62191524
There's always another trade. Let's do this again sometime.

62195146
>>62191524
It wasn't. Bull trap ends mid-May. Let's not pretend that all of this isn't predetermined...

62195166
>>62191524
It's going to ~$45k.

62195185
>>62191524
>$63000
It wasn't, it went down to 59k

item.title: The New AI Paradigm — 15 replies — 4.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
The New AI Paradigm

Open thread • Replies: 15 • Updated: 2026-05-06 01:33 UTC

OP

It's an undeniable fact that we have entered into a new paradigm of infinite wealth creation and prosperity, thanks to Agentic AI. Worldwide UBI will be implemented by giving everyone a small share of AI, quantum computing, and humanoid stocks, so that everyone can become millionaires thanks to this technological revolution and not have to work anymore. The GDP will increase by quadrillions of dollars thanks to AGI taking over the stock market, of which we are only now starting to see the beginning of. This is what Trump was talking about when he said we would be living in a new golden age. We're all going to be rich.

Early replies (first 3)

62194660
>>62194649
sndk trades at 12x forward earnings. its cheaper than PG

62194663
>>62194649
Intel reaches 200% of the share price it had about 6 years ago.

Back then it had about $25B of operating income. Its almost negative now.

Oh and there's 20% more shares outstanding vs 6 years ago.

The people who decided that today is the day they should go ahead and buy chip stocks are the guys who just made their 1999 money back from Cisco and Intel.

What a meme market. Closed all my chip holdings today

62194664
it's over

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62194670
>>62194663
ok boomer

62194674
>>62194663
I didn’t know such wise anons were in here

62194675
>>62194670
I'm 25, but I've been investing since 14. Guess that makes me a boomer here.

62194747
>>62194649
lol normies still think people are getting ubi. because how else would 8 billion humans survive right? they cant just let 8 billion humans starve right? thats like, not gonna happen right?

62194776
>>62194649
Truth OP

62194780
>>62194674
He's wrong, the market/chips like Intel and AMD and SNDK will all go another 20% before they see pullback.

62194781
>>62194663
Also this market can't crash because too much is invested in AI and the boomer portfolios need to stay up. They will turn the dollar to the peso before the market crashes

62194787
>>62194776
Except for Trump, who is a jew-owned fag (Bubba blower)

62194788
>>62194747
prot twist: the elites will let 8 billion nigas starve, but guys like Zucc will end up being mulched because their bunker isn't as secure as they thought and it tunrs out "kill all the people who weren't related to insider jews trading our fake markets" isn't a good idea and the massacre of talent, brains or skill makes it impossible to maintain the robotics they planned to rely on.

62194815
>>62194649
Yes the silicon valley technocrats WILL share their wealth and NOT create a new class of unemployable people.
We WILL see them SHARE the wealth with everyone. It WILL be a new utopia!
AI is the SAVIOR!

You people don't actually believe this right?

62194826
>>62194660
What

62194908
>>62194649
Keep up ir get left behind

>>62194781
I'm sure goldchads will appreciate it, stocklets on the other hand...

>>62194815
Savior of the jews

item.title: If you have $20k+ savings, a paid off car, and sporadic(only a 1 year gap due to helping a family member with serious he… — 14 replies — 4.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
If you have $20k+ savings, a paid off car, and sporadic(only a 1 year gap due to helping a family member with serious he…

Open thread • Replies: 14 • Updated: 2026-05-06 01:25 UTC

OP

If you have $20k+ savings, a paid off car, and sporadic(only a 1 year gap due to helping a family member with serious health) work experience, what states in the USA should I look/go to for a fresh start?

Something with potential in the next 10-15 years.

I absolutely need to move, but dont wanna fuck it up.

Early replies (first 3)

62189097
>>62188586
Maybe consider San Francisco because youre gay.

62189121
>>62188586
Skills?
Interests?
Education?

62189673
>>62188586
Florida. Boomers have the money and all the boomers are here

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62189682
>>62188586
Find a job first

62190286
>>62189673
Lol thats where in leaving, this place is cooked

>>62189121
No education besides HS

Skills, extremely proficient with computers, self learning, strong trouble shooting skills, all my employers have noted I have great work ethic, enjoy labour to an extent(fuck things like roofing, painting outside in heat) can work in shit conditions barring FL summers outdoors, great in cold, regularly was outside in 0 or below. Enjoy aspects of operations and logistics. People have also said id be good with sales. Good eye for detail. I dont want to go IT, id like to avoid a degree right now but open to any certs. Already planning to get my osha10 and forklift in the coming months before I move.

Interested in really anything that isnt soul crushing tedious office shit, or dogshit like a cashier. Hence why I avoided them since teens.

>>62189682
Worded my post kinda badly, I do and can use it as a perfect position to move around in the company, I just dont want to commit to this place as a career. The growth and destination isnt for me. Not my kind of product. I can use it though for a secured move though, then go to something else.

62190294
>>62188586
West Virginia is the next ethnostate.

62190300
go pan for gold

62192226
>>62190286
Well, I live on Ohio. It's fairly cheap.

62192241
>>62188586
Trucker in the Pacific Northwest

62192812
>>62192226
isnt an indian about to win the state, thus bringing in like 30000% more?

62193727
>>62192812
Hopefully. I voted for him.

62193983
>>62188586
Nobody ever starved in Texas. Fat of the land.

62194651
>>62188586
>bulb ID
FEED VALLEY

62194884
>>62193727
>voted for a jeet scammer
he will redeem your state

item.title: BTC Short — 6 replies — 4.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
BTC Short

Open thread • Replies: 6 • Updated: 2026-05-06 03:17 UTC

OP

Should I short 10, 20 or 40x on BTC at 82k-83k to ride all the way down to 50-40k?

Early replies (first 3)

62195067
just 2x short when it goes over ATH and enjoy risk free money instead of worrying about all the other shit

62195070
>>62195063
vertical line should be written "POSSIBLE DAY OF THE BOTTOM"

62195085
>>62195067
Sadly I don't have that much money right now :(. WIll do in 2029

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62195202
>>62195063

Go all in at 100x.

I want you to lose it all as quickly as possible and keep us updated.

62195216
>>62195063
Never go all in.

62195220
>>62195063
Bull trap can go all the way to $95k-ish and still be a bull trap, so don't get liquidated like a fag.
